data.currentPriceSource: Not available in local filings - no 8-K earnings files found. Last known repurchase price: $27.05-$31.85 (Oct-Nov 2025) per 10K-2025.htm Item 5
data.sharesSource: 10K-2025.htm, dei:EntityCommonStockSharesOutstanding as of 2026-02-06: Class A 585,458,698 + Class B 79,679,925 = 665,138,623 total
data.industry: Social Media / Digital Advertising / Visual Discovery Platform

moat: Pinterest is an AI-powered visual search and discovery platform positioned at the intersection of search, social, and commerce (10K-2025.htm, Item 1 Overview).

The platform has 619 million monthly active users globally as of December 31, 2025, with women comprising roughly two-thirds of users and Gen Z users representing over 50% of the user base (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).

Pinterest possesses a proprietary 'Taste Graph' - billions of associations between Pins, searches, boards, products and users - which generates unique first-party intent-based signals that power AI recommendation systems (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).

Over 90% of searches on Pinterest are unbranded, positioning the platform uniquely as a visual discovery tool where users come with high commercial intent before brand selection (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).

Pinterest competes primarily with Amazon, Meta (Facebook, Instagram), Google (YouTube, Lens, Gemini), OpenAI (ChatGPT), Snap, Reddit, TikTok and X - all significantly larger companies with greater resources (10K-2025.htm, Item 1, Competition).
Intellectual property includes approximately 400 issued patents and pending applications, over 660 registered trademarks as of December 31, 2025 (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).
Revenue model is entirely advertising-based (~100% from ads), with substantial concentration in retail and CPG verticals (10K-2025.htm, Item 1A Risk Factors).
Free cash flow was $1,251.9 million in 2025 with cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities of $2,467.2 million (10K-2025.htm, Item 7 MD&A Overview).
management: William Ready is CEO; Ben Silbermann (co-founder) is non-executive Chair of the Board (10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors - Key Personnel).
Executive officers' employment is at-will (10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors - Key Personnel).

Company executed a $2.0 billion stock repurchase program authorized on November 5, 2024, with share buybacks at $27.05-$31.85/share in Oct-Nov 2025 (10K-2025.htm, Item 5 Purchases of Equity Securities).
Headcount was 5,265 as of December 31, 2025 (10K-2025.htm, Item 7 MD&A Overview of 2025 results).
Board authorized entry into a definitive agreement to acquire tvScientific, Inc. in Q4 2025 (10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors - Acquisitions).
Share-based compensation expense was $880.5 million in 2025, an increase of $114.7 million year-over-year (10K-2025.htm, Item 7 MD&A Overview).
No dividends declared or paid; revolving credit facility places limitations on dividends (10K-2025.htm, Item 5).
industry: Pinterest operates in digital advertising, competing with major technology platforms including Meta, Google, Amazon, TikTok, and emerging AI competitors including OpenAI (10K-2025.htm, Item 1, Competition).
Revenue seasonality: advertising business is strongest in Q4, resulting in higher Q4 revenue and higher Q1 free cash flow from Q4 collections (10K-2025.htm, Item 1A).
Pinterest generates substantially all revenue from advertising, dependent on attracting/retaining advertisers and scaling internationally (10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors).
Digital advertising competition is intense; most advertisers do not have long-term commitments with Pinterest (10K-2025.htm, Item 1A Risk Factors).
General economic uncertainty, including inflation, tariffs, and related trade measures are cited as key risk factors for the advertising industry (10K-2025.htm, Risk Factors Preamble).
Upper funnel 'brand' revenue billed on CPM/CPV; lower funnel 'performance' revenue on CPC/CPM/CPV or other performance metrics (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).
consumer: 619 million monthly active users (MAUs) as of December 31, 2025, up 12% from December 31, 2024 (10K-2025.htm, Item 7 MD&A Overview).
Weekly Active Users (WAUs) represent approximately 62% of MAUs as of December 31, 2025 (10K-2025.htm, Item 7 Trends in User Metrics).
Users come to Pinterest for inspiration-to-action journeys - moving from idea discovery through curation to purchase - aligning with the advertiser marketing funnel (10K-2025.htm, Item 1 Overview).
Platform resonates particularly with women (~2/3 of users) and Gen Z (>50% of user base), with global reach across 100+ countries (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).

Billions of searches and saves per month, with the vast majority of queries being visual. Users use platform for home, fashion, recipes, weddings and other aspiration/shopping categories (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).
Content includes images, videos, collages, and product catalogs uploaded by retailers, brands, creators, publishers and users. Over 90% of searches are unbranded (10K-2025.htm, Item 1).
Platform historically sees lower engagement in Q2 and higher engagement in Q4 (holiday shopping season) (10K-2025.htm, Item 1A).
Revenue per user varies significantly by geography - U.S. and Canada generates highest ARPU due to relative size and maturity of digital advertising market (10K-2025.htm, Item 7).
